versions.version_description: Flawless pop-funk fusion from Diana and Nile here. Brilliant use of modulation. Please follow the record to understand the pace of these changes as there are many passing chords and nuances. Choruses play as intro. Outro plays as into repeating to fade over funk guitar figures.

tuning: E A D G B E
content:
Album Diana (1980). Song written by Nile Rodgers and Bernard Edwards (Chic).

You can dress up the Gm chords by making them Gm7, the C chords by making them C7 
and the F chords by making them F6 or F7, but the root chords should be correct.


[Intro]
[ch]Gm[/ch] [ch]C[/ch] [ch]F[/ch] [ch]Gm[/ch]  [ch]Bb[/ch] [ch]Bdim[/ch] [ch]C[/ch]  [ch]F[/ch] [ch]Gm[/ch]

[Verse 1]
[tab]        [ch]Gm[/ch]                 [ch]C[/ch]
I said, upside down you're turning me[/tab]
[tab]       [ch]F[/ch]             [ch]Gm[/ch]
You're giving love instinctively[/tab]
[tab][ch]Bb[/ch]              [ch]Bdim[/ch]   [ch]C[/ch]          [ch]F[/ch] [ch]Gm[/ch]
Round and round you're turning me[/tab]

[Chorus]
[tab][ch]Gm[/ch]     [ch]C[/ch]    [ch]F[/ch]         [ch]Gm[/ch]
Upside down, boy, you turn me[/tab]

Inside  out and round and round[/tab]
